edges.description: Progressive CD4 T-cell depletion causes severe cellular immunodeficiency.
Loss of CD4-positive T-cell populations contributes to decreased lymphocyte counts.
Defective cellular immunity permits opportunistic pathogens to cause AIDS-defining infections.
Hematologic abnormalities are common in HIV/AIDS, including anemia.
Opportunistic infection susceptibility in AIDS includes Pneumocystis pneumonia.
Opportunistic fungal infection susceptibility in AIDS includes recurrent Candida infection.
CNS HIV reservoirs and neuroinflammation produce neurocognitive impairment, including AIDS dementia complex.
Loss of immune surveillance in AIDS permits AIDS-defining Kaposi sarcoma.

nodes.description: Weakened immune surveillance increases risk of certain cancers.

evidence.reference_title: AIDS-related malignancies.
evidence.snippet: In acquired immunodeficiency due to the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), HIV itself rarely directly causes cancer; rather, it provides the immunologic background against which other viruses can escape immune control and induce tumors.

evidence.reference_title: Effect of innate and adaptive immune mechanisms on treatment regimens in an AIDS-related Kaposi's Sarcoma model.
evidence.snippet: Kaposi Sarcoma (KS) is the most common AIDS-defining cancer, even as HIV-positive people live longer. Like other herpesviruses, human herpesvirus-8 (HHV-8) establishes a lifelong infection of the host that in association with HIV infection may develop at any time during the illness.

evidence.reference_title: Immunosuppression and risk of cervical cancer.
evidence.snippet: A markedly increased risk of cervical cancer is known in women immunosuppressed due to AIDS or therapy following organ transplantation.

evidence.reference_title: Is anaemia frequent in HIV/AIDS patients presenting to a tertiary care hospital?
evidence.snippet: Anaemia was a common finding among human immune deficiency virus / acquired immunodeficiency syndrome patients.

evidence.reference_title: Experience with epoetin alfa and acquired immunodeficiency syndrome anemia.
evidence.snippet: Anemia is common in patients infected with the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV).

evidence.reference_title: Hematologic abnormalities in the acquired immune deficiency syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Ten patients were anemic, eight leukopenic, and three thrombocytopenic.

evidence.reference_title: Severe anemia is an important negative predictor for survival with disseminated Mycobacterium avium-intracellulare in acquired immunodeficiency syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Anemia was significantly more profound in patients with AIDS and MAI than in the other patients.

nodes.description: HIV infects and destroys CD4 T-cells, critical for immune system function.

evidence.reference_title: CD4(+) T-cell depletion in HIV infection: mechanisms of immunological failure.
evidence.snippet: The hallmark of acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) pathogenesis is a progressive depletion of CD4(+) T-cell populations in close association with progressive impairment of cellular immunity and increasing susceptibility to opportunistic infections (OI).

evidence.reference_title: Overview of HIV and AIDS: biology and epidemiology of the virus.
evidence.snippet: HIV-1 infects mononuclear cells using the CD4+ molecule... ongoing cellular destruction, leading to the characteristic immunodeficiency of AIDS and its opportunistic infections and neoplasms.

evidence.reference_title: HIV infection and AIDS.
evidence.snippet: The entry of one HIV virion into a human being has the potential to cause death by the inexorable replication of the virus within the principal T lymphocyte, the CD4+ T cell.

evidence.reference_title: New insights on the perturbations of T cell cycle during HIV infection.
evidence.snippet: The role of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) in the pathogenesis of the Acquired Immune-Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS) is changed. Direct HIV-mediated killing of CD4(+) T cells is not the only mechanism leading to lymphocyte depletion.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of Glutathione in the Management of Cell-Mediated Immune Responses in Individuals with HIV.
evidence.snippet: HIV is a major cause of death worldwide... HIV-positive individuals also demonstrate diminished glutathione (GSH) levels which allows for increased viral replication and...slowed the decline of CD4+ T cell counts in HIV-positive individuals.

nodes.description: HIV invades the CNS early, establishing persistent reservoirs in microglia and macrophages that contribute to neuroinflammation and neurocognitive impairment.

evidence.reference_title: Brain microglia serve as a persistent HIV reservoir despite durable antiretroviral therapy.
evidence.snippet: These data demonstrate that MG harbor replication-competent HIV and serve as a persistent reservoir in the brain.

evidence.reference_title: Neuropathogenesis of HIV: from initial neuroinvasion to HIV-associated neurocognitive disorder (HAND).
evidence.snippet: Recent work suggests that the stage for HIV neuropathogenesis may be set with initial viral entry into the CNS, followed by initiation of pathogenetic processes including neuroinflammation and neurotoxicity, and establishment of local, compartmentalized HIV replication that may reflect a tissue reservoir for HIV.

evidence.reference_title: Oral candidiasis.
evidence.snippet: Candidiasis is, by far, the most common mycotic infection of the human oral cavity... Extensive use of antibiotics and immunosuppressive drugs have greatly increased the number of Candida-induced oral infections.

evidence.reference_title: Invasive fungal infections among inpatients with acquired immune deficiency syndrome at a Chinese university hospital.
evidence.snippet: Candida albicans accounted for 57.4% of fungal pathogens isolated.

evidence.reference_title: Trends and patterns of opportunistic diseases in Danish AIDS patients 1980-1990.
evidence.snippet: Esophageal candidiasis was reported in one fifth of the patients and were reported twice as often in women as in homosexual men.

evidence.reference_title: Candidiasis.
evidence.snippet: Candidiasis.

evidence.reference_title: Predictive value of oral candidiasis as a marker of progression to AIDS.
evidence.snippet: an oral examination was carried out on a group of 200 HIV-infected patients...  ...Of the 86 (54.8%) patients with OC, 48.2% progressed to AIDS...

evidence.reference_title: Nationwide survey of neurologic manifestations of acquired immunodeficiency syndrome in Japan.
evidence.snippet: Of 1854 HIV-1 carriers, 578 had acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) and 166 (28.7% of AIDS patients) had neurologic manifestations including HIV dementia (11.8%).

evidence.reference_title: AIDS dementia-related psychosis: is there a window of vulnerability?
evidence.snippet: Psychiatric manifestations that are the direct result of HIV infection are usually seen in the setting of HIV-associated dementia.

evidence.reference_title: The acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) dementia complex.
evidence.snippet: The acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) dementia complex is a frequent and devastating complication of infection with human immunodeficiency virus-type 1 (HIV-1).

nodes.description: Multiple host restriction factors inhibit HIV replication through distinct molecular mechanisms, but are counteracted by viral accessory proteins (Vif, Vpu, Nef).
Progressive loss of CD4 T-cells leads to severe immunodeficiency.

evidence.reference_title: CD4(+) T-cell depletion in HIV infection: mechanisms of immunological failure.
evidence.snippet: The hallmark of acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) pathogenesis is a progressive depletion of CD4(+) T-cell populations in close association with progressive impairment of cellular immunity and increasing susceptibility to opportunistic infections (OI).

evidence.reference_title: Secondary immunodeficiencies: An overview.
evidence.snippet: The acquired immunodeficiency syndrome results from infections by the human immunodeficiency virus, which targets CD4 T cells leading to defective immune responses.

evidence.reference_title: Presentation of Kaposi's sarcoma in the acquired immune deficiency syndrome.
evidence.snippet: One manifestation of this disease is Kaposi's Sarcoma. Usually a rare tumor, it occurs with a high incidence in patients with AIDS.

evidence.reference_title: Disseminated Kaposi sarcoma with epithelioid morphology in an HIV/AIDS patient: A previously unreported variant.
evidence.snippet: Kaposi sarcoma is an oligoclonal HHV-8-driven vascular proliferation... We report a 52-year-old Caucasian man with HIV/AIDS and Kaposi sarcoma.

evidence.reference_title: The AIDS epidemic.
evidence.snippet: New cutaneous (e.g., seborrheic dermatitis, onychomycosis, and tinea pedis) and systemic... markers for progressive HIV infection, such as Kaposi's sarcoma...

evidence.reference_title: A human immunodeficiency virus-seronegative acquired immunodeficiency syndrome patient with opportunistic infections: A case report.
evidence.snippet: Delayed or missed diagnosis of HIV infection leads to a lack of timely therapy, resulting in rapid disease progression with opportunistic infections or malignancies.

evidence.reference_title: Pathology of acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) in children.
evidence.snippet: These disorders include nodal and extranodal lymphoproliferative lesions... smooth muscle tumors (SMTs), Kaposi's sarcoma...

evidence.reference_title: CD4(+) T-cell depletion in HIV infection: mechanisms of immunological failure.
evidence.snippet: The hallmark of acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) pathogenesis is a progressive depletion of CD4(+) T-cell populations in close association with progressive impairment of cellular immunity and increasing susceptibility to opportunistic infections (OI).

nodes.description: Impaired immunity allows opportunistic pathogens to cause infections.

evidence.reference_title: Impaired immunity in AIDS. The mechanisms responsible and their potential reversal by antiviral therapy.
evidence.snippet: The inability of CD4+ T cells of HIV-1-infected patients to mount an effective immune response is widely believed to explain the increased susceptibility of these patients to opportunistic infections.

evidence.reference_title: AIDS-associated parasitic diarrhoea.
evidence.snippet: Opportunistic parasitic infection can cause severe morbidity and mortality. Because many of these infections are treatable, an early and accurate diagnosis is important.

evidence.reference_title: Update on the pathology of AIDS.
evidence.snippet: The acquired immune deficiency syndrome (AIDS) is the result of a human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection damaging the cell-mediated immune system. ... A wide range of opportunistic infections (Ols) and tumours develop; additionally, HIV directly damages some organs.

evidence.reference_title: [Treatment and prevention of opportunistic infections].
evidence.snippet: Incidence as well as morbidity and mortality of opportunistic infections (OI) have declined remarkably since the availability of antiretroviral treatment (ART).

evidence.reference_title: Secondary immunodeficiencies: An overview.
evidence.snippet: The acquired immunodeficiency syndrome results from infections by the human immunodeficiency virus, which targets CD4 T cells leading to defective immune responses.

evidence.reference_title: Mycobacterial disease, immunosuppression, and acquired immunodeficiency syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The number and severity of such infections have increased markedly with the emergence of the acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) epidemic.

evidence.reference_title: Acquired immunodeficiency syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The occurrence of unusual infections, in particular Pneumocystis carinii pneumonia...

evidence.reference_title: Laboratory findings and clinical characteristics of Pneumocystis pneumonia and tuberculosis infection among HIV-infected patients with pulmonary infiltrates in Jakarta, Indonesia.
evidence.snippet: Pneumocystis pneumonia (PCP) and pulmonary tuberculosis infection (PTB) are important opportunistic infections in HIV-infected patients.

evidence.reference_title: Pneumocystis: immune recognition and evasion.
evidence.snippet: Pulmonary infection caused by the opportunistic fungal organism Pneumocystis continues to be a leading AIDS defining illness.

evidence.reference_title: The 'pulmonary diseases spectrum' in HIV infected children.
evidence.snippet: ...consistent use of Pneumocystis prophylaxis in all HIV exposed/infected children under 5 years of age has considerably reduced associated infections overall and respiratory infections in particular.

evidence.reference_title: The AIDS epidemic.
evidence.snippet: The nature of the clinical presentation of HIV infection continues to evolve over time....Pneumocystis carinii pneumonia...can now be added to the classic clinical markers for progressive HIV infection.
